steps.title: Find the two unit factor
steps.content: The factor $c$ is the multiplier when $x$ increases by $2$. Starting with $f(x)=35(3)^{4x}$, increasing $x$ by $2$ increases the exponent by $8$, so the multiplier is $$c=3^8=6{,}561$$ This is the constant factor the question asks to see as a base or coefficient.

steps.title: Rewrite using that factor
steps.content: Use $6{,}561=3^8$ to group the exponent in chunks of $8$: $$35(3)^{4x}=35(3^8)^{x/2}=35(6{,}561)^{x/2}$$ This equivalent form keeps the same function values for every $x$ and displays the two-unit multiplier directly as the base.

steps.title: Match the displayed base
steps.content: Choice A shows base $3$, choice B shows base $9$, and choice C shows base $81$. Choice D shows base $6{,}561$, which is the value of $c$ found from the two-unit increase. The answer is D.
